headlines.title: US Vows Fresh 50% Tariff on Canada as New Trade War Looms - Bloomberg.com

ripple_effect.name: Major tariff escalation / trade war

historical_precedents.what_happened: The U.S. imposed tariffs on hundreds of billions of dollars of Chinese goods, and China retaliated with its own tariffs, including on U.S. agricultural exports.
historical_precedents.market_impact: U.S. soybean exports to China fell sharply, and the U.S. government paid out tens of billions of dollars in subsidies to farmers hurt by the retaliation; global manufacturers reported higher input costs and supply-chain uncertainty through the period.

historical_precedents.what_happened: The U.S. raised tariffs on over 20,000 imported goods to some of the highest levels in a century; Canada, France, and other major trading partners retaliated with their own tariffs within months.
historical_precedents.market_impact: World trade volumes fell by roughly two-thirds over the following four years; widely cited by economists as having deepened and prolonged the Great Depression globally, not just in the U.S. — the clearest historical example that a major tariff escalation's damage isn't confined to the two countries directly involved.

historical_precedents.event: China's rare earth export restrictions on Japan
historical_precedents.what_happened: After Japan detained a Chinese trawler captain following a collision near the disputed Senkaku/Diaoyu Islands, China informally slowed rare earth mineral shipments to Japan without declaring a formal ban.
historical_precedents.market_impact: China's rare earth exports fell roughly 77% year-on-year by September 2011, and global prices spiked several-fold (cerium oxide rose from around $4.70/kg in April 2010 to around $36/kg that October). The U.S. filed a WTO case in 2012; the WTO ruled against China in 2014, and China dropped its export quotas in 2015 — but the episode had already pushed Japan and other importers to diversify sourcing and boost recycling.

possibility_chains: New tariffs have historically raised the cost of the affected imported goods, at least part of which has often been passed on to consumers or absorbed as lower profit margins by importers.
Retaliatory tariffs targeting a country's agricultural or manufacturing exports have historically hurt the specific industries and regions most exposed to that export market.

Extended periods of tariff uncertainty have historically led some companies to shift or diversify supply chains toward other countries, a process that has typically played out over one to several years, not weeks.

headlines.title: Ebola outbreak in DRC worsens as death toll rises to 930, WHO warns of rapid spread

ripple_effect.name: Disease outbreak with international health emergency

historical_precedents.what_happened: The World Health Organization declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern as Ebola spread across Guinea, Liberia, and Sierra Leone; several airlines suspended routes to the region and some countries imposed travel restrictions.
historical_precedents.market_impact: The World Bank estimated the outbreak cost the three hardest-hit countries over $2 billion in lost economic output.

historical_precedents.what_happened: The WHO declared a Public Health Emergency as Zika spread across the Americas, linked to birth defects; several countries issued travel advisories affecting tourism, notably ahead of the Rio de Janeiro Olympics.
historical_precedents.market_impact: Tourism-dependent economies in affected regions reported measurable declines in visitor bookings during the advisory period.

possibility_chains: A WHO-declared public health emergency has historically triggered travel advisories and, in some cases, temporary route suspensions by airlines.
Disease outbreaks with international travel warnings have historically reduced tourism and business travel to the affected region for the duration of the advisory.
Outbreaks affecting agricultural or manufacturing regions have historically disrupted local labor supply and production.
Vaccine and treatment developers have historically seen increased investment and expedited regulatory review during major outbreaks.

typical_channels: Grocery and restaurant prices commonly move with underlying crop and livestock costs.

Countries that rely on imported food have historically been more exposed to price swings than major producers.
